steps.title: Identify the key exclusion
steps.content: The passage states “All but one of the 20 moons are thought to be remnants of bodies that orbited Saturn until they broke apart in collisions.” This single statement is the decisive clue: it identifies one exceptional moon that is not a collision-remnant.

steps.title: Match to the correct answer
steps.content: Choice D says the moon “has a prograde orbit and may not be a remnant of an earlier body that orbited Saturn.” The passage confirms both elements: the moon orbits in the planet's direction (prograde), and as the one exceptional moon excluded from “all but one are remnants,” it's the one not derived from a collision. The other three either misidentify the orbit type, assign a collision origin to the exception, or claim it shares other moons' origins. That matches choice D — D is correct.
